title: Keep the module dependency graph acyclic
description: Prevent and break import cycles between modules so build, test, and reasoning stay tractable and ownership stays clear.
body: Use when adding a dependency between modules or reviewing the import graph. Do not introduce a back-edge that makes two modules import each other directly or transitively. Actions: inspect the dependency graph for cycles, break a cycle by extracting the shared abstraction into a lower layer or inverting the dependency with an interface, and verify the graph is a DAG after the change. Validation signals include a cycle-detection tool reporting zero cycles and modules building independently. Risk boundary: cycles cause fragile builds, hidden coupling, and untestable units; if a cycle cannot be cleanly broken, escalate the boundary design rather than papering over it with lazy imports.
